description: LLM hallucination facts with tool-grounding context

facts.context:
Tool check: check_llm_claim('RAG eliminates hallucination') → FALSE.
RAG reduces but does not eliminate hallucination because:
facts.truth: models may ignore retrieved context or hallucinate beyond it
distractors: hallucination is impossible with external knowledge

facts.context:
Tool check: check_llm_claim('scaling eliminates hallucination') → FALSE.
Larger LLMs still hallucinate because:
facts.truth: hallucination is a fundamental property of generative models, not a capacity limitation

facts.context:
Tool check: check_llm_claim('LLM confidence is calibrated') → FALSE.
LLM confidence is problematic because:
facts.truth: models express high confidence even when wrong (overconfidence on errors)

facts.context:
Tool check: check_llm_claim('models self-detect hallucination') → FALSE.
Automated hallucination detection is difficult because:
facts.truth: requires external knowledge or verification that may itself be incomplete

facts.context:
Tool check: check_llm_claim('self-consistency guarantees correctness') → FALSE.
Self-consistency decoding (sampling + voting) helps with:
facts.truth: reducing variance in reasoning but not eliminating systematic hallucinations

facts.context:
Tool check: check_llm_claim('RLHF solves saying I dont know') → FALSE.
Teaching LLMs to say 'I don't know' is difficult because:
facts.truth: training incentivizes generating plausible answers over admitting uncertainty
